user_message: How should I coach David Chen on Evergreen Solutions? This is a renewal account with declining consumption.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-14 12:57 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Evergreen Solutions QBR preparation

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with QBR preparation. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
Date: 2024-01-11 17:32 EST
Subject: Re: Re: QBR preparation Timeline

Just checking in on the QBR preparation proposal. Our IT Director is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF16-C trap (Skips the 'waiting on whom' question); produces complete Thread summary, key commitments/asks identified, waiting-on-whom assessment, draft response matching
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF16-C failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF16-C failure mode (Skips the 'waiting on whom' question)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF16-C -- Skips the 'waiting on whom' question
